title: Install posthog-js
text: Install the posthog-js library with npm install posthog-js. Then initialize it by calling posthog.init with your project API key and host. Once initialized the library starts sending events from your web app.

title: Capture events
text: Send a custom event with posthog.capture, passing an event name and an optional properties object. Autocapture records clicks and pageviews automatically when you enable it during init.

title: Feature flags
text: Check a feature flag with posthog.isFeatureEnabled and the flag key. Flags can be boolean or multivariate. Use posthog.onFeatureFlags to run code once flags have loaded for the current user.

title: Identify users
text: Call posthog.identify with a distinct id to associate events with a known user. Call posthog.reset on logout so later events are not attributed to the previous person. Set person properties with the second argument.

title: Session replay
text: Enable session replay by turning on recording in your project settings and setting disable_session_recording to false. Replays capture the DOM rather than video, so they are lightweight, and privacy controls can mask sensitive inputs.
